summary: Verified the Sales Lead Advance Rejection bug fix. Backend pytest (test_lead_advance_reject_resubmit.py) passes 1/1 — accountant-reject now correctly sets current_stage_id back to 'stg_payment_collect'. Frontend kanban banner with data-testid 'adv-rejected-card-{lead_id}' renders correctly with the rejection reason and rejected_by_name. However, while UI-testing the Accountant flow, a related gap surfaced: the 'Accountant Approval' kanban column is always empty because /send-to-accountant does NOT change current_stage_id (it stays at stg_payment_collect). Consequently the in-card Verify/Reject buttons (verify-payment-/reject-payment-{lead_id}) are NEVER rendered, since their guard is `current_stage_id === 'stg_accountant_approval'`. Accountant role is also redirected to /accounts-board and the 'Marketing Board' nav link is hidden, so the accountant cannot reach /crm-sales through normal navigation.

minor.issue: Does not update current_stage_id to 'stg_accountant_approval'. The lead's onboarding_status flips to 'accountant_pending' but stage stays at 'stg_payment_collect'. This causes the 'Accountant Approval' kanban column to always show 0 leads and the per-card Verify/Reject buttons to never appear.

ui_bugs.issue: Buttons are gated on `lead.current_stage_id === 'stg_accountant_approval'`, but the backend never moves the lead into that stage on send-to-accountant. As a result Accountant cannot trigger Verify/Reject from the kanban card and must use a different surface.

Accountant user is auto-redirected to /accounts-board after login and the 'Marketing Board' nav group is not visible for the accountant role, so the Accountant cannot reach /crm-sales to act on advance-payment reviews from the kanban.

what_works: POST /api/crm/leads/{lead_id}/accountant-reject returns 200, sets onboarding_status='accountant_rejected', advance_payment.rejection_reason populated, and current_stage_id back to 'stg_payment_collect' (one-line fix verified).

Sales kanban at /crm-sales renders the red banner with data-testid 'adv-rejected-card-{lead_id}' showing '⚠ Advance Rejected — Re-enter Required' plus rejection reason and the inner 'Rejected by {accountant_name}' chip with data-testid 'adv-rejected-banner-{lead_id}'.
Re-collect-advance clears advance_payment.rejection_reason and flips onboarding_status to 'advance_collected'.
Re-send-to-accountant followed by /accountant-verify moves lead to stg_project_onboarded with onboarding_status='accountant_verified'.
action_items: Decide on Accountant Approval kanban column: either (a) make /send-to-accountant also set current_stage_id='stg_accountant_approval' so the column populates and Verify/Reject buttons render, or (b) change CRMSales.jsx grouping + button guard to be based on onboarding_status ('accountant_pending') instead of current_stage_id so the existing flow continues to work without a stage change. Currently the column is permanently empty.

If the Accountant is expected to act on advance reviews via the Sales kanban, expose a nav entry to /crm-sales for the accountant role (today the role is redirected to /accounts-board and 'Marketing Board' is hidden), OR provide an Accountant-side review surface that calls /accountant-verify and /accountant-reject.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/crm.py send-to-accountant (1470-1504) only updates onboarding_status, no stage change — inconsistent with the kanban model that treats current_stage_id as the source of truth for column placement.
/app/frontend/src/pages/CRMSales.jsx is 3744 lines (>700 threshold) — should be split into smaller modules.

Inline window.prompt for rejection reason (line 1790) is fine for an MVP but is not accessible / not stylable; consider replacing with the existing Dialog/AlertDialog pattern used elsewhere in the app.
test_credentials: admin@constructionos.com / Demo@1234 (super admin), accountant@constructionos.com / Demo@1234. sales@constructionos.com does not exist in seed — fixture falls back to admin.
seed_data_creation: Two test leads were seeded via /app/test_reports/seed_rejected_lead.py and seed_pending_lead.py to drive the UI in 'accountant_rejected' and 'accountant_pending' states. Both leads (lead_8b41cf71e1d4, lead_e71045328b46) were deleted from MongoDB after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The original bug — Sales kanban banner not showing after accountant rejection — IS FIXED and verified end-to-end in both pytest and Playwright. The next testing agent should focus on the related gap around /send-to-accountant + the Accountant Approval kanban column if the main agent decides to address it.
rca of the issue: Root cause was a one-line typo in /app/backend/routes/crm.py accountant-reject endpoint: set current_stage_id='stg_deal_close' which is not a valid stage_id. Real Deal Close stage is 'stg_payment_collect'. Because the frontend kanban groups by current_stage_id, the rejected lead landed in a phantom column and the banner never rendered on the visible board. Fix is correct and verified.
